humanSummary.text: Final audit found no P0/P1 blockers for KAIFUU-034. Remaining archive-report semantics and detector coverage issues are tracked as non-blocking DAG follow-ups.

findings.title: Archive-only matches can look adapter-matched
findings.description: Archive-only unsupported inputs can currently make the top-level report look adapter-matched instead of separating adapter status from archive detection status.

evidence.detail: Final wave 12 audit recorded adapter-status versus archive-status separation as a P2 follow-up with no P0/P1 completion blocker.

findings.impact: Operators can read archive recognition as adapter support, causing unsupported packed inputs to appear more actionable than they are.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Top-level adapter status remains distinct from archive detection status so archive-only unsupported inputs do not make the whole report look adapter-matched.
Archive-only unsupported inputs report a separate archive detection status with stable semantic capability diagnostics.

acceptanceCriteria: Top-level adapter status remains distinct from archive detection status so archive-only unsupported inputs do not make the whole report look adapter-matched
Archive-only unsupported inputs report a separate archive detection status with stable semantic capability diagnostics
auditFocus: Archive-only matches shown as adapter support
Unsupported packed inputs hidden behind matched status
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Tracked as planned DAG node KAIFUU-046 for wave 12 completion bookkeeping.

findings.title: Marker-only subtype signals need normalization or unknown-variant diagnostics
findings.description: Marker-only subtype signals and requirements can remain attached to non-detected rows instead of being normalized away or emitted as explicit unknown-variant diagnostics.

evidence.detail: Final wave 12 audit recorded marker-only subtype signal handling as a P3 follow-up with no P0/P1 completion blocker.

findings.impact: Non-detected rows can carry subtype or key-requirement hints that look like detected variants even when the detector did not establish support.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Marker-only subtype signals and key requirements on non-detected rows are normalized away or reported as unknown-variant diagnostics.
Unknown-variant diagnostics identify the marker family without implying adapter support or a known decryptable subtype.

acceptanceCriteria: Marker-only subtype signals and key requirements on non-detected rows are normalized away or reported as unknown-variant diagnostics
Unknown-variant diagnostics identify the marker family without implying adapter support or a known decryptable subtype
auditFocus: Marker-only subtype metadata implying detected variants
Unknown variants reported as known key requirements
existingDagNodeUpdate.notes: Tracked as planned DAG node KAIFUU-046 for wave 12 completion bookkeeping.

findings.title: RPG Maker encrypted suffix coverage is representative only
findings.description: Committed regression coverage instantiates representative RPG Maker MV/MZ encrypted suffixes, but does not instantiate every encrypted suffix.

evidence.detail: Final wave 12 audit recorded full RPG Maker MV/MZ encrypted suffix fixture coverage as a P3 follow-up with no P0/P1 completion blocker.

findings.impact: A suffix-specific regression could escape if only representative encrypted asset suffixes are tested.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Committed regression fixtures instantiate every RPG Maker MV/MZ encrypted suffix and assert stable diagnostics for each suffix.
Representative suffix helper code cannot accidentally drop a supported suffix without a failing detector regression.

acceptanceCriteria: Committed regression fixtures instantiate every RPG Maker MV/MZ encrypted suffix and assert stable diagnostics for each suffix
Representative suffix helper code cannot accidentally drop a supported suffix without a failing detector regression
auditFocus: RPG Maker encrypted suffix coverage gaps
Representative suffix helpers hiding missing committed cases
